critical_issue: All 5 test files have broken pytorch_reference: missing return, using input instead of input_tensor

description: Reader+Writer passthrough for dim=-1. Identity copy c_0 to c_16.

approach: TensorAccessor sequential tile reads, prepare_reduce_scaler for c_2, Wt tiles per row
Identity passthrough: copy_tile from c_0 to c_16, one tile at a time per row
Generic tile writer: TensorAccessor sequential tile writes from c_16 to DRAM

upstream_fixes: Fixed all 5 test pytorch_reference functions: missing return statements and wrong variable names

description: Add exp_tile to each tile in compute_w. Output = exp(input).

approach: Added exp_tile_init and exp_tile(0) after copy_tile to apply exp to each tile in DST before packing

cb_summary: c0:1push/1wait_per_tile c2:1push/persistent c16:1push/1wait_per_tile (same as stage1, only DST ops changed)

description: Full unstable softmax dim=-1: exp + reduce_sum(REDUCE_ROW) + recip + mul. Uses helpers for reduce and binary ops.

details: NoAccumulation not in scope, needs compute_kernel_lib:: prefix

change: Qualified NoAccumulation with compute_kernel_lib:: namespace prefix

details: Max diff 0.29, first row matches but later rows diverge. Test ref is stable softmax, but our kernel is unstable - may be expected bf16 precision issue with unstable softmax

description: SCALAR broadcast in Phase 3/4 is wrong: REDUCE_ROW output has per-row values in col0, not a single scalar. Need COL broadcast for sub and mul to apply per-row values across width. With Wt=1 the single tile still has 32 rows each with their own sum.
evidence: Output diverges per-row: first row matches (same reciprocal applied) but later rows use wrong normalization factor

change: Changed Phase 4 mul broadcast from SCALAR to COL. REDUCE_ROW output is COL-shaped (col0 valid per row), not a single scalar.

design_deviations: Changed broadcast from SCALAR to COL for Phase 4 mul - design was wrong about SCALAR for REDUCE_ROW output
